Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Afroman Wins Civil Trial over Use of Police Raid Footage in His Music Videos 92 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. How many branches can your CPU predict? 20 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. OpenRocket 640 upvotes, 112 comments
  4. Show HN: I built 48 lightweight SVG backgrounds you can copy/paste 318 upvotes, 61 comments
  5. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 146 upvotes, 28 comments
  6. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 309 upvotes, 76 comments
  7. Afroman found not liable in defamation case brought by Ohio cops who raided home 442 upvotes, 119 comments
  8. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 404 upvotes, 109 comments
  9. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 219 upvotes, 56 comments
  10. Cook: A simple CLI for orchestrating Claude Code 248 upvotes, 70 comments
  11. Astral to Join OpenAI 178 upvotes, 56 comments
  12. Pretraining Language Models via Neural Cellular Automata 20 upvotes, 3 comments
  13. The next fight over the use of facial recognition could be in the supermarkets 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 171 upvotes, 66 comments
  15. Juggalo Makeup Blocks Facial Recognition Technology 39 upvotes, 12 comments
  16. 'Your Frustration Is the Product' 87 upvotes, 35 comments
  17. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 474 upvotes, 245 comments
  18. RX – a new random-access JSON alternative 119 upvotes, 53 comments
  19. Warranty Void If Regenerated 429 upvotes, 252 comments
  20. OpenAI to Acquire Astral 86 upvotes, 41 comments
  21. LotusNotes 125 upvotes, 65 comments
  22. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 165 upvotes, 92 comments
  23. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 51 upvotes, 23 comments
  24. Twelve-Tone Composition 15 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. Nvidia NemoClaw 342 upvotes, 222 comments
  26. OpenBSD: PF queues break the 4 Gbps barrier 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  27. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 52 upvotes, 28 comments
  28. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 126 upvotes, 118 comments
  29. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 634 upvotes, 746 comments
  30. Iran war energy shock sparks global push to reduce fossil fuel dependence 128 upvotes, 136 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
